---
title: "Polymarket Tmax Strategy - Frozen Deployment Report (Config D3)"
author: "Oscar Ovanger"
date: 2026-07-07
format:
  pdf:
    number-sections: true
    pdf-engine: pdflatex
    fontsize: 10pt
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{setspace}
          \setstretch{1.05}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{hyperref}
          \hypersetup{colorlinks=true, linkcolor=blue, urlcolor=blue, citecolor=blue}
          \setlength{\floatsep}{6pt}
          \setlength{\textfloatsep}{8pt}
          \setlength{\intextsep}{6pt}
          \setlength{\abovecaptionskip}{4pt}
          \setlength{\belowcaptionskip}{2pt}
engine: jupyter
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
from __future__ import annotations

import json
import subprocess
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib

matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

PROJECT_ROOT = Path.cwd().parent if Path.cwd().name == "reports" else Path.cwd()
ANALYSIS_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "data" / "analysis"
D3_TRADES_DIR = ANALYSIS_DIR / "pace_amendment_d3_trades"
FIGURE_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "reports" / "figures"
FIGURE_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

DATA_PATHS = {
    "lambda_calibration": ANALYSIS_DIR / "lambda_calibration.csv",
    "convective_sweep": ANALYSIS_DIR / "convective_sweep.csv",
    "joint_validation": ANALYSIS_DIR / "joint_validation.csv",
    "pace_amendment": ANALYSIS_DIR / "pace_amendment.csv",
    "two_piece_calibration": ANALYSIS_DIR / "two_piece_calibration.csv",
    "freeze_live_trades": ANALYSIS_DIR / "freeze_live_trades.csv",
    "freeze_live_unsettled": ANALYSIS_DIR / "freeze_live_unsettled.csv",
    "deploy_config": PROJECT_ROOT / "config" / "deploy_config.json",
    "model_config": PROJECT_ROOT / "models" / "ngboost_v2" / "model_config.json",
}

PALETTE = {
    "blue": "#4878CF",
    "orange": "#E68A2E",
    "grey": "#8A8A8A",
    "green": "#2E7D32",
    "red": "#C62828",
}

plt.rcParams.update(
    {
        "figure.dpi": 130,
        "axes.spines.top": False,
        "axes.spines.right": False,
        "axes.grid": True,
        "grid.alpha": 0.25,
        "font.size": 10,
    }
)

VERIFICATION: list[str] = []
CORRECTIONS: list[str] = []
UNVERIFIED: list[str] = []


def read_csv(path: Path) -> pd.DataFrame | None:
    if not path.exists():
        UNVERIFIED.append(f"source file absent: {path}")
        return None
    return pd.read_csv(path)


def check_value(label: str, draft: float | int | str, data_val: float | int | str, tol: float = 0.011) -> str:
    VERIFICATION.append(f"{label}: draft={draft}, data={data_val}")
    if isinstance(draft, str):
        return str(data_val)
    d = float(draft)
    v = float(data_val)
    if abs(d - v) <= tol:
        return f"{v:.3f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".") if isinstance(draft, float) else str(int(v))
    CORRECTIONS.append(f"{label}: {draft} -> {v}")
    return f"{v:.3f}".rstrip("0").rstrip(".") if abs(v) < 100 else f"{v:.2f}"


def emit_figure(
    relative_path: Path,
    caption: str,
    label: str,
    *,
    width: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    rel = relative_path.name if relative_path.parent == FIGURE_DIR else relative_path.as_posix()
    if not rel.startswith("figures/"):
        rel = f"figures/{relative_path.name}"
    escaped = caption.replace('"', "'")
    width_attr = f" width={width}" if width else ""
    print(f'![]({rel}){{#{label}{width_attr} fig-cap="{escaped}"}}')


def save_and_emit(
    fig,
    filename: str,
    caption: str,
    label: str,
    *,
    width: str | None = None,
) -> None:
    path = FIGURE_DIR / filename
    fig.savefig(path, bbox_inches="tight", dpi=160)
    plt.close(fig)
    emit_figure(path, caption, label, width=width)


def missing_figure(path: str, filename: str, caption: str, label: str) -> None:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 2))
    ax.axis("off")
    ax.text(0.5, 0.5, f"DATA MISSING: {path}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=12, color="red")
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, filename, caption, label)


pa_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["pace_amendment"])
lam_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["lambda_calibration"])
cv_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["convective_sweep"])


def load_d3_trades() -> pd.DataFrame:
    frames = []
    for window in ("early", "middle", "late"):
        path = D3_TRADES_DIR / f"d3_{window}_trades.jsonl"
        if not path.exists():
            UNVERIFIED.append(f"D3 trades absent: {path}")
            continue
        rows = [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text().splitlines() if line.strip()]
        frames.append(pd.DataFrame(rows))
    if not frames:
        return pd.DataFrame()
    return pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)


d3 = load_d3_trades()


def show_table(df: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    """Emit dataframe for Quarto cross-referenceable tables."""
    display(df)


try:
    FREEZE_HASH = subprocess.check_output(
        ["git", "rev-parse", "--short", "HEAD"], cwd=PROJECT_ROOT, text=True
    ).strip()
except Exception:
    FREEZE_HASH = "unknown"
    UNVERIFIED.append("git rev-parse --short HEAD failed")

RENDER_DATE = date.today().isoformat()
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-cities
#| tbl-cap: "Deployed cities and ASOS settlement stations."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

city_rows = [
    ("Atlanta", "KATL", "America/New_York", "Southeast"),
    ("Austin", "KAUS", "America/Chicago", "South"),
    ("Chicago", "KORD", "America/Chicago", "Midwest"),
    ("Dallas", "KDAL", "America/Chicago", "South"),
    ("Houston", "KHOU", "America/Chicago", "South"),
    ("Miami", "KMIA", "America/New_York", "Southeast"),
    ("New York", "KLGA", "America/New_York", "Northeast"),
    ("Seattle", "KSEA", "America/Los_Angeles", "Pacific Northwest"),
]
cities_df = pd.DataFrame(city_rows, columns=["City", "Station", "Timezone", "Region"])
show_table(cities_df)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-features
#| tbl-cap: "NGBoost v2 feature columns (from model artifact feature_columns)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

model_cfg = json.loads(DATA_PATHS["model_config"].read_text())
feature_cols = model_cfg["feature_columns"]
purpose = {
    "hrrr_tmax": ("HRRR 10Z", "Deterministic Tmax prior from HRRR (fxx>=1)"),
    "peak_cloud_cover": ("HRRR 10Z", "Peak-window mean total cloud cover"),
    "peak_solar_flux": ("HRRR 10Z", "Peak-window solar flux"),
    "snow_depth": ("HRRR 10Z", "Snow depth covariate"),
    "temp_early_morning": ("ASOS", "Early-morning station temperature"),
    "nwp_tmax_openmeteo": ("Open-Meteo", "Open NWP Tmax prior"),
    "tmax_lag1": ("WU history", "Previous-day Tmax"),
    "tmax_lag2": ("WU history", "Two-day Tmax lag"),
    "tmax_roll3": ("WU history", "3-day rolling mean Tmax"),
    "tmax_roll7": ("WU history", "7-day rolling mean Tmax"),
    "hrrr_error_lag1": ("Derived", "Prior-day HRRR minus observed error"),
    "lgb_tmax_pred": ("Stage-1 LGBM", "LightGBM stack point forecast"),
    "station_id": ("Static", "City/station identifier embedding"),
    "latitude": ("Static", "Station latitude"),
    "elevation": ("Static", "Station elevation"),
    "doy_sin": ("Calendar", "Day-of-year sine"),
    "doy_cos": ("Calendar", "Day-of-year cosine"),
}
feat_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        {"Feature": c, "Source": purpose.get(c, ("", ""))[0], "Purpose": purpose.get(c, ("", ""))[1]}
        for c in feature_cols
    ]
)
show_table(feat_df)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-lambda
#| tbl-cap: "Lambda calibration by window (edge threshold 0.037)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

lam_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["lambda_calibration"])
if lam_df is not None:
    show = lam_df[
        ["window", "lambda", "n_trades", "mean_p_eff", "win_rate", "calibration_gap", "trades_per_day"]
    ].copy()
    show = show.rename(columns={"calibration_gap": "cal_gap", "mean_p_eff": "mean_p_eff"})
    for col in ("mean_p_eff", "win_rate", "cal_gap", "trades_per_day"):
        show[col] = show[col].map(lambda v: f"{v:.3f}")
    # verify headline gaps at lambda=0.35
    for w, draft in [("early", -0.009), ("middle", 0.014), ("late", 0.006)]:
        val = float(lam_df[(lam_df["window"] == w) & (lam_df["lambda"] == 0.35)]["calibration_gap"].iloc[0])
        check_value(f"lambda 0.35 cal_gap {w}", draft, val, tol=0.002)
    show_table(show)
else:
    display(Markdown("_DATA MISSING: lambda_calibration.csv_"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-convective
#| tbl-cap: "Convective threshold sweep (late window); early and middle invariant."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

cv_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["convective_sweep"])
if cv_df is not None:
    late = cv_df[cv_df["window"] == "late"].copy()
    late["threshold"] = late["threshold"].fillna("null")
    show = late[
        [
            "threshold",
            "n_trades",
            "trades_per_day",
            "win_rate",
            "avg_realized_edge",
            "n_city_days_skipped",
        ]
    ]
    for w, draft in [("null", 0.032), ("0.5", 0.045), ("0.4", 0.046)]:
        val = float(late[late.threshold.astype(str) == w]["avg_realized_edge"].iloc[0])
        check_value(f"convective late edge {w}", draft, val, tol=0.002)
    show_table(show)
else:
    display(Markdown("_DATA MISSING: convective_sweep.csv_"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-joint
#| tbl-cap: "Joint validation grid (lambda=0.35)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

jv_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["joint_validation"])
if jv_df is not None:
    show = jv_df.copy()
    elim = {
        "A": "late min_br $79.05; late tpd 1.29",
        "B": "late min_br $79.05",
        "C": "late tpd 1.12",
        "D": "late tpd 1.16",
    }
    show["eliminated_reason"] = show["config_id"].map(elim)
    show_table(show)
else:
    display(Markdown("_DATA MISSING: joint_validation.csv_"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-pace
#| tbl-cap: "Pace amendment variants (Config D base)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

if pa_df is not None:
    show = pa_df.copy()
    verdict = {
        ("D0", "late"): "eliminated (tpd 1.16)",
        ("D1", "late"): "eliminated (tpd 1.347 < 1.35)",
        ("D2", "late"): "survivor",
        ("D3", "late"): "survivor; selected",
        ("D4", "late"): "eliminated (tpd 1.16)",
    }
    show["verdict"] = show.apply(lambda r: verdict.get((r["variant"], r["window"]), ""), axis=1)
    d3_rows = pa_df[pa_df["variant"] == "D3"]
    check_value("D3 early final", 137.15, d3_rows[d3_rows["window"] == "early"]["final_bankroll"].iloc[0], tol=0.02)
    check_value("D3 middle final", 142.93, d3_rows[d3_rows["window"] == "middle"]["final_bankroll"].iloc[0], tol=0.02)
    check_value("D3 late final", 110.01, d3_rows[d3_rows["window"] == "late"]["final_bankroll"].iloc[0], tol=0.02)
    show_table(show)
else:
    display(Markdown("_DATA MISSING: pace_amendment.csv_"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-fallback
#| tbl-cap: "D3 fallback versus primary realized edge by window."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

if pa_df is not None:
    d3_fb = pa_df[pa_df["variant"] == "D3"][
        [
            "window",
            "n_fallback_trades",
            "fallback_win_rate",
            "fallback_mean_realized_edge",
            "primary_mean_realized_edge",
        ]
    ].copy()
    show_table(d3_fb)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-rejected
#| tbl-cap: "Tested and rejected alternatives."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

rej_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        ("Kelly sizing", "2026-06/07", "Uncalibrated probabilities; drawdown amplification; 5-contract floor"),
        ("Student-t NGBoost", "2026-06", "Near-Gaussian df; CRPS 6.6 vs 1.18; bucket hit collapsed"),
        ("Two-piece Gaussian", "2026-07-07", "PIT worsened (KS p 0.068 to 0.000); marginal cal gap gain"),
        ("Settlement-anchoring contrarian", "2026-06", "Naive rule EV -$0.009/trade despite WU-CLI bias"),
        ("Seasonal lambda", "2026-07-07", "Miscalibration season-independent; one global lambda preferred"),
        ("Top-3-per-day (D4)", "2026-07-07", "Does not fix zero-candidate days"),
        ("Sequential Bayesian fusion", "Deferred", "No private D-1 signal; calibration binds not fusion"),
    ],
    columns=["Alternative", "Date rejected", "Reason"],
)
show_table(rej_df)
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-live
#| tbl-cap: "Settled live trades at freeze (order-log reconciliation)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

live_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["freeze_live_trades"])
unset_df = read_csv(DATA_PATHS["freeze_live_unsettled"])
if live_df is not None:
    show = live_df.copy()
    show["entry"] = show["entry"].map(lambda v: f"{v:.3f}")
    show["pnl"] = show["pnl"].map(lambda v: f"{v:+.2f}")
    wins = int(live_df["won"].sum())
    n = len(live_df)
    pnl = float(live_df["pnl"].sum())
    summary = pd.DataFrame(
        [{"date": "SUMMARY", "city": f"{n} settled", "bucket": f"{100*wins/n:.1f}% win", "entry": "—", "pnl": f"{pnl:+.2f}", "won": ""}]
    )
    show = pd.concat([show, summary], ignore_index=True)
    CORRECTIONS.append(f"live settled summary: draft 17/52.9%/+6.25 -> data {n}/{100*wins/n:.1f}%/{pnl:+.2f}")
    show_table(show)
    if unset_df is not None and len(unset_df):
        display(Markdown("**Unsettled fills at freeze**"))
        show_table(unset_df)
else:
    display(Markdown("_DATA MISSING: freeze_live_trades.csv_"))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-config
#| tbl-cap: "Frozen deployment configuration (deploy config JSON)."
#| tbl-pos: "H"

cfg = json.loads(DATA_PATHS["deploy_config"].read_text())
redact_keys = {"log_dir", "sigma_source", "model_dir"}
rows = []
for k, v in cfg.items():
    if k in redact_keys:
        v = Path(str(v)).name if k != "log_dir" else "logs/"
    rows.append({"Key": k, "Value": json.dumps(v) if isinstance(v, (list, dict)) else v})
rows.append({"Key": "freeze_commit", "Value": FREEZE_HASH})
rows.append({"Key": "render_date", "Value": RENDER_DATE})
cfg_df = pd.DataFrame(rows)
show_table(cfg_df)
```

## Abstract

This report documents the frozen production strategy for the MCP 60-day live
trading challenge on Polymarket same-day maximum temperature (Tmax) markets,
deployed 2026-07-08 with 49 days remaining and account equity $84.98. The
strategy combines an NGBoost distributional forecast with a stack of trading
rules selected through pre-registered validation gates: probability shrinkage
toward market prices (lambda = 0.35), rolling per-city bias correction,
a convective-day skip filter, exclusion of marine-layer cities, flat 5-contract
sizing, an anti-cyclic daily risk budget, and a pace fallback mechanism that
guarantees trade-count compliance. On three non-overlapping 49-day windows of
real Polymarket order book data the frozen configuration finished at $137.15,
$142.93, and $110.01 from an $84.98 start, never breached an $80 internal
floor, and sustained 1.73 trades per day in the hardest (summer) window.
The report records the full selection history, including two candidate
configurations eliminated by pre-registered gates and five tested-and-rejected
model improvements, and states known limitations honestly: parts of the rule
stack were tuned on the same evaluation windows, and backtested maker fills
are optimistic relative to live execution.

## 1. Challenge Constraints and Objective

The MCP challenge imposes: 100 pUSD starting capital, a minimum of 80 trades
over 60 days, a maximum single-position size of 30% of capital, and automatic
elimination if equity touches 70 pUSD. The primary evaluation metric is the
Sharpe ratio, with Sortino, maximum drawdown, return on capital, and
consistency as secondary metrics.

At the freeze date (2026-07-07, day 11), the live record is 23 trades placed,
17 settled at a 52.9% win rate, +$6.25 realized PnL^[corrected from draft at render: order-log reconciliation yields 22 settled, 27.3% win rate, -$15.43; see @tbl-live], and account equity of
$84.98. The remaining requirement is therefore 57 trades in 49 days, a
minimum pace of 1.16 trades per day. The strategic objective for the
remainder of the challenge is ordered: (1) never approach the $70 elimination
line, (2) satisfy the 80-trade minimum with margin, (3) harvest the residual
summer edge identified in Section 8. The configuration described here is
frozen: the only sanctioned changes are a hard de-risk if equity falls below
$78, or the repair of a demonstrated live-versus-backtest behavioral bug.

## 2. Market Structure and Settlement

Polymarket offers same-day Tmax markets as neg-risk event groups. Each
city-date is a set of mutually exclusive binary YES/NO contracts, one per
2 degree F bucket (e.g. 84-85F) with LESS_THAN and GREATER_THAN tails. Exactly
one bucket resolves YES at $1.00; all others resolve NO at $0.00.

Settlement uses Weather Underground, which reports the maximum of hourly
METAR spot readings at each city's ASOS station:

$$
T_{WU}(i,d) = \max_{h} T_{METAR}(i,d,h) \tag{1}
$$

This is not the continuous daily maximum published in the NWS CLI product;
a systematic bias of roughly +1F (CLI above WU) was confirmed across all
trading cities. All model targets and settlement logic in this system use
IEM-reconstructed WU values, verified against Polymarket resolution on 15 of
15 audited city-dates (settlement audit, June 2026).

Fees: maker orders (GTC limit, post_only) pay zero fees; taker fees are
C x 0.05 x p x (1-p). All orders in this strategy are maker orders placed at
best_ask - $0.01. The exchange minimum order size is 5 contracts, which
binds the sizing design in Section 6.

@tbl-cities

Two of the ten available cities are excluded (Section 5.4): Los Angeles
(KLAX) and San Francisco (KSFO).

## 3. Forecast Model

The deployed forecast is the NGBoost v2 global Gaussian model, unchanged
since June. It is a distributional regression producing per-prediction
(mu, sigma), trained on 2021-2024, validated on 2025, tested on 2026+, with
Wunderground Tmax as the target. Features (17): hrrr_tmax, peak_cloud_cover,
peak_solar_flux, snow_depth (HRRR 10Z cycle, fxx >= 1 leakage guard);
temp_early_morning (ASOS); nwp_tmax_openmeteo; tmax_lag1, tmax_lag2,
tmax_roll3, tmax_roll7; hrrr_error_lag1; lgb_tmax_pred (stage-1 LightGBM
stack); station_id, latitude, elevation, doy_sin, doy_cos. Sigma carries a
global calibration multiplier fitted on validation (k approximately 1.20).

@tbl-features

The model's point accuracy on the 2026 test set is MAE 1.82F with a modal
bucket hit rate of 32.6%. Two diagnostic findings from June shaped everything
downstream. First, an apparent 15pp accuracy gap versus the earlier TrackB
per-city models was shown to be a scoring artifact (continuous versus
rounded +-1F windows; the rounded metric places NGBoost at 52.8%, inside the
TrackB band). Second, the market's modal bucket settles YES at roughly 44%
versus the model's 33%: the market, aggregating all public NWP plus intraday
information, is a better point forecaster than the model. The strategy
therefore does not attempt to out-forecast the market; it extracts value from
the model's residual disagreement under heavy shrinkage, disciplined risk
rules, and structural conditions (zero maker fees) that make small edges
tradeable.

## 4. From Forecast to Bucket Probabilities

Four transformations connect the raw model output to tradeable probabilities.

Static settlement bias. The raw mu targets a mixture of historical
conventions; a fixed per-city median offset b_WU aligns it to the WU
settlement scale:

$$
\mu_1 = \mu_{NGB} - b_{WU} \tag{2}
$$

Rolling bias correction. A per-city exponentially weighted moving average of
recent residuals adapts to regime drift:

$$
b_t = \alpha r_t + (1-\alpha)b_{t-1}, \quad \alpha = 1 - 0.5^{1/20} \tag{3}
$$

with r_t = mu_1 - T_WU on settled days, the correction clipped to +-1.5F,
and residuals appended automatically at settlement:

$$
\mu_{adj} = \mu_1 - \mathrm{clip}(b_t, -1.5, +1.5) \tag{4}
$$

An earlier ablation showed this correction is load-bearing: removing it cost
roughly 1.4 Sharpe points in the v5b backtest.

Bucket probabilities. Gaussian CDF with a half-degree continuity correction
at integer boundaries. For a RANGE bucket [a, b]:

$$
p_k = \Phi\!\left(\frac{b + 0.5 - \mu_{adj}}{\sigma}\right) - \Phi\!\left(\frac{a - 0.5 - \mu_{adj}}{\sigma}\right) \tag{5}
$$

with one-sided forms for tail buckets, renormalized to sum to one over the
listed buckets. A two-piece (skewed) Gaussian alternative was implemented and
rejected (Section 9).

Probability shrinkage. The decisive calibration step. The effective
probability blends the model with the market price c:

$$
p_{eff} = \lambda p_k + (1-\lambda)c, \quad \lambda = 0.35 \tag{6}
$$

Section 8.1 documents why 0.35: at the previously deployed lambda = 0.6 the
system was overconfident in every one of three evaluation windows (calibration
gaps +0.044, +0.062, +0.041); lambda in {0.25-0.35} closes the gap in all
three. The value is global. A seasonal switch was considered and rejected as
an unnecessary second parameter once the miscalibration proved
season-independent.

## 5. Trade Selection Rules

5.1 Entry and edge. At approximately 10:10 local time, for each active city
the best tradeable bucket maximizes edge e = p_eff - c subject to a price
floor c >= 0.15, with maker entry c = best_ask - 0.01. A trade qualifies if
e >= E* = 0.037. Note that under lambda = 0.35 this threshold implies a raw
model-market disagreement of at least (0.037 / 0.35) = 0.106, so qualifying
trades are large-disagreement events by construction.

5.2 Daily selection. Qualifying candidates are ranked by edge; the top 2
across all cities are taken (max_trades_per_day = 2).

5.3 Convective skip filter. During June-August, any city-day whose HRRR
peak-window mean total cloud cover is >= 0.5 is skipped before selection.
Rationale: summer convection caps heating asymmetrically; the symmetric
Gaussian overprices buckets above mu on exactly these days. Marine-stratus
cities (SF, LA, Seattle) are exempt from this particular filter because
persistent marine cloud is not convective; SF and LA are excluded outright
regardless (5.4). The threshold sweep (Section 8.2) showed monotone
improvement in late-window realized edge (0.032 to 0.045 at threshold 0.5,
47 city-days skipped) with early and middle windows untouched.

5.4 City set. Eight cities: Atlanta, Austin, Chicago, Dallas, Houston, Miami,
New York, Seattle. Los Angeles and San Francisco are excluded on mechanism:
marine-layer burnoff timing is a documented HRRR weakness, the model carries
no feature capable of resolving it, and both cities were net negative in
every evaluation window. The exclusion is causal, not PnL-mined; the grid
result (Section 8.3) confirmed it dominates on every quality metric.

5.5 Pace fallback. If, after normal selection, fewer than 2 trades qualified
on a day, the best remaining candidate with e > 0.01 is added, repeating
until the day has 2 trades or candidates are exhausted. Fallback trades
respect every other rule (price floor, convective skip, exclusions, budget,
sizing) and are tagged entry_mode = "fallback" in all logs. This mechanism
exists solely to satisfy the 80-trade constraint without loosening
selectivity on normal days; empirically (Section 8.4) fallback trades were
approximately EV-neutral-to-positive, and in the summer window outperformed
primary trades.

5.6 Exit. Hold to settlement. Zero maker fees, wide spreads, and 8-city
diversification favor settlement over intraday profit-taking in this
configuration; the profit-target exit validated on Kalshi was not carried
over after the fee model changed.

## 6. Risk and Capital Management

Sizing. Flat 5 contracts per trade, unconditional. The exchange minimum is
5 contracts, so no smaller size exists; an earlier reduced-sizing rule
(3 contracts below $85) was discovered to be unplaceable and was removed from
every code path, with assertions n_contracts == 5 at all order-construction
sites. Capital at risk per trade is 5c dollars (about $1.00-$3.00 at typical
entries).

Daily budget (anti-cyclic). The daily capital-at-risk cap is

$$
\mathrm{cap}(B) = \frac{\min(B, 100) - 70}{5} \tag{7}
$$

At B = $84.98 the cap is $3.00. The min(B, 100) term prevents pro-cyclical
scaling after wins; the divisor-5 form ties risk to the distance from
elimination. If the next candidate trade would push the day's total capital
at risk past the cap, that trade is dropped whole (contract counts are never
reduced, by the exchange minimum).

Elimination bound. Because positions opened on day d settle from day d
observations, at most one day's exposure is unresolved at any time, and the
daily loss is bounded by cap(B). The distance to elimination therefore
contracts by at most a factor 4/5 per maximum-loss day:

$$
B_{d+1} - 70 \geq \tfrac{4}{5}(B_d - 70) \tag{8}
$$

Under strict rule adherence the bankroll cannot reach $70 in finite time from
capped daily losses alone; the residual elimination risks are operational
(fill anomalies, rule bugs, the one-day settlement lag in the bankroll input
to Eq. 7) rather than statistical. Internal soft limits: hard de-risk review
below $78; the validation gates required min bankroll >= $80 in every
window.

```{python}
#| label: fig-d3-equity
#| output: asis

cap = "D3 trade-level bankroll by window from $84.98 start."
if d3.empty:
    missing_figure(str(D3_TRADES_DIR), "frozen_d3_equity.png", cap, "fig-d3-equity")
else:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
    colors = {"early": PALETTE["blue"], "middle": PALETTE["orange"], "late": PALETTE["grey"]}
    for window, grp in d3.groupby("window"):
        grp = grp.sort_values(["day_index", "date"])
        bankroll = [84.98]
        for pnl in grp["pnl_usd"]:
            bankroll.append(bankroll[-1] + float(pnl))
        ax.plot(range(len(bankroll)), bankroll, label=window, color=colors.get(window, "black"), linewidth=1.6)
    ax.axhline(70, color=PALETTE["red"], linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label="Elimination ($70)")
    ax.axhline(80, color=PALETTE["grey"], linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label="Internal floor ($80)")
    ax.axhline(100, color=PALETTE["green"], linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label="Recovery target ($100)")
    ax.scatter([0], [84.98], color="black", s=20, zorder=5)
    ax.set_xlabel("Trade number")
    ax.set_ylabel("Bankroll (USD)")
    ax.legend(loc="best", fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_d3_equity.png", cap, "fig-d3-equity", width="100%")
```

## 7. Validation Methodology

All rule selection was evaluated on 1,107+ city-dates^[corrected from draft: 1,107+ -> 963 rows in `reports/backtest_eligible_dates.csv`] of real Polymarket
order book snapshots (Feb-Jun 2026), replayed through the production code
path (identical selection, sizing, budget, and bias-correction logic;
maker fills assumed at best_ask - 0.01). Three non-overlapping-in-character
49-day windows, each restarted from $84.98 with a fresh rolling-bias cache:
early (2026-02-03 to 2026-03-23), middle (2026-03-18 to 2026-05-08), late
(2026-05-04 to 2026-06-24). The late window is the stress case and the
closest analog to the July-August deployment season.

Selection discipline. Every configuration decision after 2026-07-07 was made
by a pre-registered rule written down before the run: elimination gates
(min bankroll >= $80 in every window; trades/day >= 1.30, raised to 1.35 for
the final run to reflect July convection), then ranking by late-window
realized edge, then a fixed tie-break order, with final bankroll explicitly
excluded as a selection criterion. Two full candidate grids were run under
this protocol; the first eliminated all four candidates (Section 8.3), which
is reported as a result, not an inconvenience.

Definitions. Computed edge = p_eff - entry price. Realized edge = settlement
outcome (1 or 0) - entry price, averaged over trades. The calibration gap
(computed minus realized) measures the sum of model miscalibration and
market efficiency; a well-calibrated book has a gap near zero.

## 8. Results

8.1 Lambda calibration. On 251 settled backtest trades with recovered raw
probabilities, the deployed lambda = 0.6 produced positive calibration gaps
in all three windows; the gap-minimizing lambdas were 0.35 (early), 0.25
(middle), 0.35 (late). At lambda = 0.35 the gaps are -0.009, +0.014, +0.006.
The overconfidence was not seasonal, so a single global value was adopted.

@tbl-lambda

```{python}
#| label: fig-lambda-cal
#| output: asis

cap = "Calibration gap versus shrinkage lambda by evaluation window."
if lam_df is None:
    missing_figure(str(DATA_PATHS["lambda_calibration"]), "frozen_lambda_cal.png", cap, "fig-lambda-cal")
else:
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.5))
    for win, color in zip(["early", "middle", "late"], [PALETTE["blue"], PALETTE["orange"], PALETTE["grey"]]):
        sub = lam_df[lam_df["window"] == win].sort_values("lambda")
        ax.plot(sub["lambda"].values, sub["calibration_gap"].values, marker="o", label=win, color=color)
    ax.axhline(0, color="black", linewidth=0.8)
    ax.axvline(0.35, color=PALETTE["green"], linestyle=":", linewidth=1.2, label="lambda=0.35")
    ax.set_xlabel("lambda")
    ax.set_ylabel("Calibration gap")
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_lambda_cal.png", cap, "fig-lambda-cal", width="100%")
```

8.2 Convective filter sweep. Thresholds {null, 0.6, 0.5, 0.4} left the early
and middle windows unchanged and improved the late window monotonically:
realized edge 0.032 (null) to 0.045 (0.5) to 0.046 (0.4), with 47 and 53
city-days skipped respectively. The plateau between 0.5 and 0.4 indicates a
real effect rather than threshold-chasing; 0.5 was adopted.

@tbl-convective

```{python}
#| label: fig-convective
#| output: asis

cap = "Late-window realized edge and trades/day versus convective threshold."
if cv_df is None:
    missing_figure(str(DATA_PATHS["convective_sweep"]), "frozen_convective.png", cap, "fig-convective")
else:
    late = cv_df[cv_df["window"] == "late"].copy()
    late["threshold"] = late["threshold"].fillna("null").astype(str)
    order = ["null", "0.6", "0.5", "0.4"]
    late["threshold"] = pd.Categorical(late["threshold"], categories=order, ordered=True)
    late = late.sort_values("threshold")
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7, 3.5))
    x = np.arange(len(late))
    axes[0].bar(x, late["avg_realized_edge"], color=PALETTE["blue"])
    axes[0].set_xticks(x)
    axes[0].set_xticklabels(late["threshold"].astype(str))
    axes[0].set_title("Realized edge")
    axes[0].axvline(order.index("0.5"), color=PALETTE["green"], linestyle=":", alpha=0.8)
    axes[1].bar(x, late["trades_per_day"], color=PALETTE["orange"])
    axes[1].set_xticks(x)
    axes[1].set_xticklabels(late["threshold"].astype(str))
    axes[1].set_title("Trades/day")
    axes[1].axvline(order.index("0.5"), color=PALETTE["green"], linestyle=":", alpha=0.8)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_convective.png", cap, "fig-convective", width="100%")
```

8.3 Joint validation grid (first pre-registered run). Four configs, all with
lambda 0.35: A (threshold 0.6, 10 cities), B (0.5, 10 cities), C (0.6, 8
cities), D (0.5, 8 cities). All four were eliminated: A and B breached the
$80 floor (min bankroll $79.05, late window) with late realized edges of
0.001 and 0.020; C and D passed every risk gate comfortably (min bankroll
$83.23, middle-window realized edge 0.150) but failed pace (1.12 and 1.16
trades/day, late window). The grid separated two failure modes cleanly:
including LA/SF buys trade count with drawdown and near-zero summer edge;
excluding them produces the best book the system can write but not often
enough. Config D dominated on every quality metric and became the base for a
single, final pace amendment.

@tbl-joint

8.4 Pace amendment (second and final pre-registered run). Five variants on
base D: D0 (reference), D1 (E* 0.030), D2 (E* 0.025), D3 (E* 0.037 plus pace
fallback at min edge 0.01, target 2 trades/day), D4 (top-3/day). D0 and D4
failed pace (1.16), D1 missed the raised gate by a hair (1.347 < 1.35).
Survivors D2 and D3 tied on late realized edge (~0.059); the pre-registered
tie-break selected D3 for preserving full selectivity on normal days. D4's
failure confirmed the shortfall came from zero- and one-candidate days, which
adding a third daily slot cannot fix.

D3 final numbers: early 94 trades, 1.92/day, 35.1% win, realized edge 0.111,
final $137.15; middle 87 trades, 1.78/day, 34.5%, 0.133, final $142.93^[middle final bankroll in `pace_amendment.csv`: $142.925]; late
85 trades, 1.73/day, 25.9%, 0.059, final $110.01^[late final bankroll in CSV: $110.005]. Minimum bankroll across all
windows: $81.23 (middle). Fallback trades: 21/16/28 per window with realized
edges -0.007/+0.091/+0.106; in the summer window the fallback book
outperformed the primary book (0.106 versus 0.036), consistent with heavy
shrinkage making the marginal sub-threshold trades genuinely cheap rather
than adversely selected.

@tbl-pace
@tbl-fallback

```{python}
#| label: fig-pace-late
#| output: asis

cap = "Late-window realized edge and trades/day by pace-amendment variant."
if pa_df is None:
    missing_figure(str(DATA_PATHS["pace_amendment"]), "frozen_pace_late.png", cap, "fig-pace-late")
else:
    late = pa_df[pa_df["window"] == "late"].copy()
    variants = ["D0", "D1", "D2", "D3", "D4"]
    late = late.set_index("variant").loc[variants].reset_index()
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7, 3.5))
    x = np.arange(len(late))
    colors = [PALETTE["blue"] if v != "D3" else PALETTE["green"] for v in late["variant"]]
    axes[0].bar(x, late["mean_realized_edge"], color=colors)
    axes[0].set_xticks(x)
    axes[0].set_xticklabels(late["variant"])
    axes[0].set_title("Realized edge")
    axes[1].bar(x, late["trades_per_day"], color=colors)
    axes[1].axhline(1.35, color=PALETTE["red"], linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label="pace gate 1.35")
    axes[1].set_xticks(x)
    axes[1].set_xticklabels(late["variant"])
    axes[1].set_title("Trades/day")
    axes[1].legend(fontsize=7)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_pace_late.png", cap, "fig-pace-late", width="100%")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-d3-heatmap
#| output: asis

cap = "D3 per-city PnL (USD) by evaluation window."
if d3.empty:
    missing_figure(str(D3_TRADES_DIR), "frozen_d3_heatmap.png", cap, "fig-d3-heatmap")
else:
    heat = d3.groupby(["city", "window"], as_index=False)["pnl_usd"].sum()
    pivot = heat.pivot(index="city", columns="window", values="pnl_usd").fillna(0)
    pivot = pivot[["early", "middle", "late"]] if set(["early", "middle", "late"]).issubset(pivot.columns) else pivot
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
    vmax = max(abs(pivot.values.min()), abs(pivot.values.max()), 1)
    im = ax.imshow(pivot.values, cmap="RdYlGn", aspect="auto", vmin=-vmax, vmax=vmax)
    ax.set_xticks(range(len(pivot.columns)))
    ax.set_xticklabels(pivot.columns)
    ax.set_yticks(range(len(pivot.index)))
    ax.set_yticklabels(pivot.index)
    for i in range(len(pivot.index)):
        for j in range(len(pivot.columns)):
            ax.text(j, i, f"${pivot.values[i, j]:.1f}", ha="center", va="center", fontsize=8)
    fig.colorbar(im, ax=ax, fraction=0.03)
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_d3_heatmap.png", cap, "fig-d3-heatmap", width="100%")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-d3-drawdown
#| output: asis

cap = "D3 late-window drawdown from running peak bankroll."
late_trades = d3[d3["window"] == "late"].sort_values(["day_index", "date"]) if not d3.empty else pd.DataFrame()
if late_trades.empty:
    missing_figure("d3_late_trades", "frozen_d3_drawdown.png", cap, "fig-d3-drawdown")
else:
    bankroll = [84.98]
    for pnl in late_trades["pnl_usd"]:
        bankroll.append(bankroll[-1] + float(pnl))
    s = pd.Series(bankroll)
    dd = s - s.cummax()
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3.5))
    ax.plot(dd.values, color=PALETTE["red"])
    idx = int(dd.idxmin())
    ax.annotate(
        f"Max DD ${dd.min():.2f}",
        xy=(idx, dd.min()),
        xytext=(idx, dd.min() - 2),
        arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", color="black"),
        fontsize=8,
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Trade number")
    ax.set_ylabel("Drawdown from peak (USD)")
    plt.tight_layout()
    save_and_emit(fig, "frozen_d3_drawdown.png", cap, "fig-d3-drawdown", width="100%")
```

8.5 Interpretation. Under lambda 0.35 the book changed character: fewer,
larger-disagreement trades with strongly negative calibration gaps in spring
(-0.071, -0.082: the system now underestimates its own win rate) and a small
positive gap in summer. Underconfidence costs trade count, not money, and the
pace fallback repays the count. The realistic forward expectation for the
49-day live period, applying a haircut for maker-fill optimism (Section 11),
is a finish in the $95-$120 range with worst plausible drawdowns of roughly
$20 from peak and no credible path to the $70 line under rule adherence.

## 9. Tested and Rejected Alternatives

The following were implemented, evaluated under the same protocol, and
rejected. They are recorded because negative results constrain the space as
much as positive ones.

1. Kelly sizing (half, eighth, and exact multi-bet). Rejected June-July:
   requires calibrated probabilities the system did not have where it
   mattered; pro-cyclical variants amplified drawdowns; flat sizing matched
   Sharpe with the shallowest drawdown, and the exchange 5-contract minimum
   removes most sizing freedom at this bankroll regardless.
2. Student-t distribution (NGBoost LogScore). Rejected June: learned df ~ 18
   (near-Gaussian), CRPS 6.6 versus 1.18 Gaussian, bucket hit rate collapsed.
3. Two-piece Gaussian for summer skew. Rejected 2026-07-07 by its own
   pre-registered rule: validation skew exists (sigma-down ratio 1.21 in
   summer 2025, 1.00 outside summer) but a constant-ratio correction worsened
   validation PIT (KS p 0.068 to 0.000); the late-window calibration gap
   improved only marginally. Infrastructure retained, disabled in config.
4. Settlement-anchoring contrarian trade (buy one bucket below market
   favorite, motivated by the WU-versus-CLI bias). Rejected June: market
   implied mean is +0.28F above WU actual (p = 0.00003) but settlement lands
   above the favorite (22.0%) more often than below (19.1%); expected PnL of
   the naive rule was -$0.009 per trade.
5. Seasonal (summer-only) lambda. Rejected 2026-07-07: the miscalibration at
   lambda 0.6 was present in all windows; one global parameter beats two with
   a regime boundary.
6. Top-3-per-day selection (D4). Rejected: adds trades only on days already
   rich in candidates; does not address the binding zero-candidate days.
7. Sequential Bayesian multi-lead-time framework (separate design document).
   Deferred, not built: the D-1 information set contains no private signal
   (same public NWP cycles as the market), and the challenge evidence shows
   the binding constraint is calibration and risk rules, not forecast fusion.
   The one component worth keeping, an intraday running-max truncation
   posterior, is listed as future work.

@tbl-rejected

## 10. Live Record at Freeze

23 trades placed over 11 days; 17 settled, 9 wins (52.9%), +$6.25 realized^[corrected from draft: see Section 1 and @tbl-live];
6 filled positions unsettled at the time of the freeze snapshot^[corrected from draft: 6 unsettled -> 1 per order-log reconciliation]; account
equity $84.98. The live book to date was produced under earlier
configurations (manual three-source agreement trading, then v5b parameters)
and is not a sample of the frozen strategy; it is reported for continuity and
counts toward the 80-trade requirement.

@tbl-live

## 11. Limitations and Known Risks

1. In-sample rule selection. The convective threshold and lambda were chosen
   using the same 1,107 city-dates on which the final configurations were
   evaluated. Mitigations: single-knob mechanical changes, calibration-based
   (not PnL-based) selection criteria, pre-registered gates, and a hard freeze
   after two passes. The backtested edge should nevertheless be treated as an
   upper bound.
2. Maker-fill optimism. The backtest assumes every maker order at
   best_ask - 0.01 fills. Live fills are adversely selected: resting bids
   fill preferentially when fair value moves through them. Realized live
   edge will be below backtest realized edge; the plan and gates were sized
   for this.
3. Season shift. The late window (May-June) is the closest analog to
   July-August but convection deepens; live convective skip rates will
   exceed the backtest's 47 city-days. The pace gate was raised to 1.35 and
   the fallback mechanism exists for precisely this reason.
4. One-day bankroll lag. Eq. 7 consumes the bankroll settled through
   yesterday; the elimination bound in Eq. 8 weakens marginally but remains
   a contraction.
5. Model staleness. NGBoost v2 is frozen; the rolling bias EWMA is the only
   adaptive component. A sustained regime the EWMA cannot express (shape
   rather than level) would degrade edge; the convective filter covers the
   known instance of this.
6. Single-machine operations. Cron on a laptop with VPN dependency is the
   weakest link; Section 12 and the ops runbook cover watchdogs, locks,
   preflights, and wake scheduling.
7. Backtest sizing vs exchange minimum notional. Simulations through the
   freeze assumed flat 5 contracts at all entry prices. Polymarket also
   enforces a \$1 minimum order value; at entry prices below \$0.20,
   5 contracts are below that floor and would require 6–7 contracts live.
   Re-sizing D3 late-window trades (entry \< \$0.20) to
   `max(5, ceil(1.005/price))` changes late realized edge and PnL only
   marginally versus the frozen report; see `scripts/analyze_d3_notional_resize.py`
   for per-window counts and deltas. Live sizing after 2026-07-08 matches
   the exchange rule.

## 12. Operations Summary

Daily cycle (all times US Central): 08:00 settlement of yesterday's trades
(updates bankroll file and rolling-bias residuals), 08:20 settlement
watchdog, 10:00-22:00 autotrader every 5 minutes (entry logic ~10:10 local
per city; idempotent via daily state file; lockfile prevents overlap), 10:25
trade watchdog. The first run of each day emits the full effective config,
bankroll, and git commit hash to the log and to Pushover. Live-mode refusal
conditions (no order placed, alert sent): missing or stale bankroll file,
failed CLOB/VPN preflight. Freeze policy as stated in Section 1.

## 13. Future Work (not for this challenge window)

Intraday running-max truncation posterior (conditioning bucket probabilities
on the observed morning maximum); MetNet-3 covariate once a paired archive
exists; D-1 market-efficiency study on Telonex order book data; live
dashboard analytics on the primary-versus-fallback split.

## Appendix A: Frozen configuration

@tbl-config

```{python}
#| label: verification-summary
#| echo: true

pdf_path = PROJECT_ROOT / "reports" / "frozen_strategy_report.pdf"
lines = ["## Verification summary (render-time)", "", f"Output PDF: `{pdf_path}`", ""]
lines.append(f"**Checks recorded:** {len(VERIFICATION)}")
lines.append(f"**Corrections:** {len(CORRECTIONS)}")
lines.append(f"**Unverified / missing sources:** {len(UNVERIFIED)}")
lines.append("")
if CORRECTIONS:
    lines.append("### Corrections (draft -> data)")
    for item in CORRECTIONS:
        lines.append(f"- {item}")
if UNVERIFIED:
    lines.append("### Unverified")
    for item in UNVERIFIED:
        lines.append(f"- {item}")
if VERIFICATION:
    lines.append("### Numeric claims checked")
    for item in VERIFICATION[:40]:
        lines.append(f"- {item}")
    if len(VERIFICATION) > 40:
        lines.append(f"- ... and {len(VERIFICATION)-40} more")
display(Markdown("\n".join(lines)))
```
